execution_boundary.summary: VCs express claims and proofs but do not execute transitions.

preparation_boundary.summary: Credentials may accompany prepared actions while consequence authority remains separate.

commitment_boundary.summary: Credential status and verifier policy may inform standing at commit time.

semantic_equivalence_boundary.summary: Credential schemas and claim semantics require normalized interpretation.

unknown_trajectory_boundary.summary: VCs do not classify unknown runtime trajectories.

authority_boundary.summary: Credential validity does not independently establish current delegation or execution authority.

evidence_freshness_boundary.summary: Credential status, suspension, revocation, and proof validity require freshness checks.

reconstruction_boundary.summary: Issuer, holder, verifier, credential, presentation, status, and securing method can support reconstruction.

interoperability_path.summary: VC artifacts enter as identity, claim, and status evidence.

next_required_action: add valid, revoked, and untrusted-issuer fixture cases
